title: Ringmain
wikitext:
[]: Ringmain?             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Wed Dec 21, 2005 9:39 pm I've been reading the 5.1 dialogues of episode 11 and I've seen lots of mentions about a "ringmain". I've seen on google these equipments, but I still have no idea what they are. Can anyone explain please? Thx.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Wed Dec 21, 2005 10:37 pm "Ringmain" definitely isn't an English word, and a quick scan through the script doesn't reveal what you mean. Can you post usage of the word in context?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Dec 21, 2005 11:50 pm A "ring main" is the term used in the UK at least for the main power circuit, from which current is tapped. See e.g. http://www.diydoctor.org.uk/projects/ringmain.htm

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Thu Dec 22, 2005 12:18 pm That has to be it, because all those ringmain lines have something to do with electricity, like "The main cable for the second ringmain has burn out on several places" (something like that). So strange, that word doesn't even show up on dictionary.com. Thanks a lot Tines.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Thu Dec 22, 2005 1:58 pm Mr. Tines wrote: A "ring main" is the term used in the UK at least for the main power circuit, from which current is tapped. See e.g. http://www.diydoctor.org.uk/projects/ringmain.htm

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Fri Dec 23, 2005 10:39 am New term on the same episode, seems electrical too. "Cell-motor" in the following line: C240 [...] 男「いいぞ！セルモーター、回せ！」
